title: Post-Term Pregnancy
authors: David Peleg & Yael Simpson-Lavy / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Post-Term Pregnancy?
แม้ post-term pregnancy เป็น obstetric problem เป็นหลัก แต่ neonatologist คือผู้รับทารกที่เกิดมา พร้อมความเสี่ยงที่สะสมมานาน ได้แก่: (1) Clifford postmaturity syndrome — dry cracked skin, absent vernix, meconium staining, 'old and worried' appearance; (2) Meconium aspiration syndrome (MAS) — ↑ meconium-stained AF → preparation ทีม NRP ก่อนคลอด; (3) Perinatal mortality สูงขึ้นชัดเจนตั้งแต่ ≥42 wk — stillbirth 2.664/1000 vs 0.5/1000 ที่ 39–41 wk; (4) Placental dysfunction → oligohydramnios → cord compression → fetal distress; macrosomia → shoulder dystocia → birth injuries; (5) Long-term: ↓ IQ ~2 pts, ↑ CP risk (RR 1.44), behavioral/emotional problems 2x, obesity.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย definition ของ post-term pregnancy (294 days/42 wk from LMP) และ ACOG classification: early/full/late/post-term term
ระบุข้อจำกัดของ LMP ในการ estimate GA และเหตุใด first-trimester US จึง accurate กว่า
รู้ incidence ที่กำลังลดลงและ etiologies ทั้ง genetic (ADAMTS13, X-linked placental sulfatase deficiency) + environmental (stress, obesity)
วิเคราะห์ maternal complications: macrosomia, cesarean delivery, shoulder dystocia, oligohydramnios, preeclampsia — เชื่อมกลไก placental dysfunction
อธิบาย Clifford postmaturity syndrome (1954) และ neonatal risks: MAS, asphyxia, low Apgar, NICU admission, neonatal convulsions
เข้าใจ perinatal mortality data จาก 12 million US births 2014–2016: stillbirth per 1000 ที่ GA แต่ละสัปดาห์
ระบุ long-term complications: ↓ IQ, CP risk, behavioral problems, mental disorders, obesity (particularly boys)
อธิบาย fetal surveillance methods: kick counts, NST, BPP, modified BPP, oxytocin contraction test, AFV
เปรียบเทียบ methods of induction: PGE1 (misoprostol), PGE2 (dinoprostone), Foley bulb catheter, cervical ripening balloon, membrane stripping, amniotomy
ใช้ management algorithm: 40 wk → 41 wk → 42 wk — รู้ว่าเมื่อไรแนะนำ induction เมื่อไรยัง monitor ได้
เตรียม NICU team สำหรับ post-term neonate: MAS risk, meconium management, macrosomia/birth injury, hypoglycemia, polycythemia
1. Definition and Classification

ACOG Classification — Deliveries จาก 37 สัปดาห์ขึ้นไป (Committee Opinion 579, 2013)
Early term: 37 0/7 – 38 6/7 wk. Full term: 39 0/7 – 40 6/7 wk ← ช่วง lowest adverse outcomes. Late term: 41 0/7 – 41 6/7 wk. Post-term: ≥42 0/7 wk (≥294 days from LMP). ศัพท์ที่ไม่ควรใช้: 'postdates', 'prolonged', 'postmature' — หลีกเลี่ยง confusion. 37–38+6 wk คือ early term ไม่ใช่ term — adverse outcomes สูงกว่า full term. Prior to recent evidence, 37–42 wk ถือเป็น 'term' ทั้งหมด — แต่ข้อมูลชัดว่าช่วงที่ safe ที่สุดคือ 39 0/7 – 40 6/7 wk (Reddy Obstet Gynecol 2011).
Human gestation: เฉลี่ย 266 days from fertilization หรือ 280 days (40 wk) from LMP
Post-term ใช้กับ singleton pregnancies; ไม่มี consensus ว่า twin post-term คือ GA เท่าไร; twins มักไม่เกิน 40 wk
Stillbirth risk: สูงขึ้นชัดเจนตั้งแต่ ≥42 wk — perinatal mortality data จาก 12 million births
2. Gestational Age Assessment
2.1 Limitations of LMP
LMP ขึ้นอยู่กับ (1) maternal recall; (2) regular cycle length; (3) ovulation timing — ทั้ง 3 อย่างไม่แน่นอน
Between 6–40% of women จะมีการปรับ LMP หลังทำ first-trimester US
Second/third-trimester US: discrepancy 7–14+ days — ไม่ควรใช้ dating ใน late pregnancy
ACOG Committee Opinion 700 (2017): ปรับ LMP/EDD หาก first-trimester US discrepancy ≥6 วัน
2.2 First-Trimester Ultrasound

First-Trimester US — ลด Post-Term Rate ได้จริง
Bennett KA et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2004;190:1077 (RCT): first-trimester US screening → ลด post-term labor induction rate อย่างมีนัยสำคัญ. Harrington DJ et al BJOG 2006;113:171: crown-rump length dating → ↓ rate induction for prolonged pregnancy. สรุป: first-trimester US เป็น gold standard สำหรับ pregnancy dating — ลด over-diagnosis post-term จาก LMP error.
3. Incidence and Etiology
3.1 Incidence — กำลังลดลงทั่วโลก

rows: ก่อน widespread early intervention

blocks.text: เหตุที่ rate ลดลง: early elective induction กลายเป็น common practice; better dating with first-trimester US;
3.2 Etiologies
3.2.1 Risk Factors
Nulliparity: primiparous women มีความเสี่ยงสูงกว่า
Male fetus: associated with longer gestation
Fetal anencephaly: hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis ผิดปกติ → ไม่มี signal กระตุ้น labor
Prior post-term pregnancy: recurrence risk สูง
Maternal obesity: direct correlation between obesity category and post-term birth risk (Slack Slack BMC Pregnancy Childbirth 2019;19:434)
X-linked placental sulfatase deficiency: ↓ estrogen precursors → ↓ cervical ripening → ↓ labor initiation
Adrenal hypoplasia (fetal): ↓ fetal adrenal signal → ↓ estrogen pathway → prolonged gestation
3.2.2 Genetic Factors

Familial Clustering และ ADAMTS13 Gene — Genetic Basis of Post-Term
Oberg AS et al Am J Epidemiol 2013;177:531: Swedish cohort n=475,429 births → familial clustering post-term; ~50% of variation attributable to genetic (maternal + fetal) factors. Sisters of women with post-term birth = ↑ risk. Schierding W et al J Med Genet 2018;55:55 (GWAS, Northern Finland cohorts n=9,141): 1,167 born post-term → association with intronic variant in ADAMTS13 gene. 'Post-term potential': individuals genetically predisposed to post-term labor แต่ delivered term via induction → ยังมี long-term post-term complications ได้ (Schierding BMC Res Notes 2014;7:720).
As many as 25% of routine inductions in low-risk women = due to post-term pregnancy
Some long-term risks of post-term = shared underlying genetic factors, not just prolonged gestation per se
3.2.3 Environmental Factors

Psychological stress: Margerison-Zilko Evol Med Public Health 2015: September 11, 2001 attack → ↑ odds post-term delivery among gestations 33–36 wk in September 2001 (all race/ethnic/sex groups); hypothesis: adaptive strategy — prolong delivery until environment improves or fetus hardier
Maternal obesity: direct correlation obesity category → absolute post-term birth risk — mechanism unclear; ↑ leptin/insulin resistance may alter labor signaling
4. Maternal Complications
4.1 Macrosomia, Cesarean Delivery, Shoulder Dystocia

Macrosomia Risk Escalates with GA — Alexander JAMA Data
Alexander JM et al Obstet Gynecol 2000;96:291: n>50,000 births: macrosomia (BW >4000 g) ↑ 8% (40 wk) → 12% (41 wk) → 15% (42 wk). Cesarean rate: 7% → 14%. Shoulder dystocia: known risk factor for post-term pregnancy (Gottlieb & Galan OG Clin N Am 2007;34:501). สรุปสำหรับ neonatologist: post-term macrosomic baby → เตรียม shoulder dystocia protocol + birth injury assessment ทันทีหลังคลอด.
4.2 Placental Dysfunction — กลไกหลักของ Neonatal Risk
Placenta = programmed lifespan: syncytiotrophoblast dysfunction + placental apoptosis ใน post-term period (documented histologically) → ↓ functional capacity → ↓ fetal oxygenation
Umbilical cord erythropoietin: ↑ 67% ใน post-term pregnancies — marker of chronic fetal hypoxia; เกิดจาก placental dysfunction

Oligohydramnios: ↓ amniotic fluid (mechanism debated — ↓ fetal urine + ↑ resorption); defined as deepest vertical pocket <2 cm หรือ AFI <5 cm; → cord compression → variable decelerations → fetal distress
Preeclampsia: incidence ↑ with GA — 5.0% (39 wk) → 6.9% (≥40 wk) in low-risk nulliparous women (Reddy 2011)
5. Neonatal Risks
5.1 Clifford Postmaturity Syndrome

Clifford 1954 — Clinical Description ที่ยังใช้ได้ถึงวันนี้
Clifford SH 1954: บรรยาย postmaturity syndrome ว่า: (1) absent vernix; (2) dry, cracked, parchment-like skin; (3) 'old and worried' appearance with open alert eyes; (4) ↑ alertness/irritability; (5) long thin body. Related to: in utero meconium release + placental dysfunction + intrauterine hypoxia. ปัจจุบัน: still valid description — ผิวแห้งลอกเป็นชั้น (fingernails long, yellowish from meconium staining), subcutaneous fat หายไป, umbilical cord + placenta stained with meconium.
Meconium-stained amniotic fluid: rate ↑ สูงในpost-term — thick meconium + post-term hypoxia = ↑ MAS risk
Low 5-min Apgar, asphyxia: placental insufficiency → intolerance of labor → intrapartum hypoxia/acidemia
Neonatal convulsions: ↑ incidence ใน post-term — sequela ของ HIE
NICU admission rate ↑: post-term group: more NICU admission, greater respiratory morbidity, more infections, more hypoglycemia vs term (Maoz 2019)
MAS risk: post-term + thick meconium + birth asphyxia = classic triad; prepare team สำหรับ meconium at delivery
Birth trauma: shoulder dystocia → brachial plexus injury, clavicle fracture, HIE
5.2 Perinatal Mortality Data

สรุป: perinatal mortality คงที่ที่ 38–41 wk แต่ ขึ้นชัดตั้งแต่ ≥42 wk (Po G et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2020;222:88). ข้อมูล early studies: significant ↑ stillbirth + neonatal death; later studies (well-monitored low-risk): no significant increase — likely selection bias จาก live-born registries ที่ไม่นับ intrauterine fetal death.
5.3 Specific Neonatal Complications in Post-Term

rows: In utero hypoxia → ↑ gut motility + relaxation of anal sphincter → meconium release; thick meconium + gasping = MAS
NRP team ready; สังเกต tone/breathing ก่อน suction; intubate if depressed
↓ glycogen stores (placental dysfunction), macrosomic = ↑ insulin demand, stress-induced catecholamines
Serial glucose; dextrose + feed; target >2.6 mmol/L (46 mg/dL)
Chronic hypoxia → ↑ EPO → ↑ RBC; Hct often >65% (same mechanism as FGR)
Venous Hct Day 1–2; partial exchange if symptomatic
Macrosomia → shoulder dystocia → brachial plexus, clavicle, HIE, facial nerve
Exam for crepitus, arm position, Moro asymmetry; XR if suspected fracture
Placental insufficiency → fetal intolerance of labor → acidemia
Low Apgar → cord gas → cooling criteria assessment (≥36 wk)
iNO if PPHN; surfactant for MAS-induced surfactant dysfunction
Chorioamnionitis more common in prolonged pregnancy; immunosuppression effect
Low threshold sepsis workup; ampicillin + gentamicin if risk factors

blocks.text: 6. Long-Term Complications
6.1 Neurodevelopmental Outcomes
IQ: ↓ ~2 IQ points vs term — statistically significant แต่ clinical significance ไม่ชัด; อาจ driven by severely impaired subset (birth trauma/asphyxia)
Intellectual disability: ↑ risk ใน large register-based study — but risks for CP, epilepsy, sensorineural defects: NOT consistently increased
Cerebral palsy: one study: RR 1.44 for CP after ≥42 wk gestation — statistically significant; mechanism = perinatal hypoxia
6.2 Behavioral, Psychiatric, Obesity

Long-Term Post-Term Complications — Beyond NICU
Behavioral problems: 2x higher in post-term children at 1.5 and 3 years — attention deficit/hyperactivity; emotional problems. Mental disorders: ↑ risk any mental disorder in adulthood — particularly substance use + anxiety disorders, especially in men. Obesity: post-term boys → ↑ BMI at 3 yr + older; difference increases with age. แจ้ง family ตั้งแต่ NICU: post-term neonate มี higher risk developmental + behavioral issues → refer developmental pediatrics ระยะยาว.
7. Fetal Surveillance Methods

ไม่มี RCT ที่แสดงว่า antepartum fetal testing ลด morbidity/mortality — แต่ based on ↑ fetal morbidity/mortality ที่ post-term จึง prudent ที่จะ test ไม่ช้ากว่า 41 wk โดย neonatologist ควรทราบ method เหล่านี้เพื่อ communicate กับ OB team

rows: Mother perceives movement lying quietly on left side
Reactive: ≥2 accelerations (15 bpm above baseline ≥15 sec) in 20 min
CTG with 3–5 contractions per 10 min via oxytocin infusion
US: AFI = sum of 4 quadrants (normal ≥5 cm) OR deepest pocket ≥2 cm
AFI <5 cm หรือ DVP <2 cm = oligohydramnios
US + NST: tone, gross movements, breathing, AFV, NST — each 2 pts
Normal fluid + reactive NST = reassuring; faster and simpler

blocks.text: Note (RCT BPP vs modified BPP after 42 wk): more abnormal results with BPP — possibly due to using AFI (less accurate) vs deepest vertical pocket. Modified BPP preferred for routine post-term surveillance.
8. Induction of Labor — Methods
8.1 Factors Affecting Induction Success
Successful induction: placing nonlaboring woman into active labor
Favorable cervix factors: ↑ dilatation + effacement; engaged presenting part; multiparous; ↓ BMI
Failed induction: cannot diagnose without rupture of membranes — amniotomy must have been performed

Calculator (Penn Medicine): takes cervical dilatation, effacement, station, parity, BMI, GA; example — nulliparous 40 wk, undilated, 50% effaced, station −2 → 59% success; parous 2-cm dilated, 80% effaced, station +1 → 83% success
8.2 Common Methods of Induction

rows: Fingers or breast pump, up to 5 min per breast several times/day — can stimulate endogenous oxytocin
Insert finger into cervix to loosen membranes — stimulates PGF2α release; done in office; ↑ cervical ripening; recommended at 41 wk
Vaginal tablet (Cytotec); potent cervical ripening + uterine stimulation; caution in prior uterine scar
Endocervical gel (Prepidil) OR slow-release vaginal insert (Cervidil); safer in prior scar than misoprostol
24–26 Fr; inserted into uterus; extra-amniotic balloon inflated 30–80 mL saline; mechanical ripening; low infection risk
Double lumen 18 Fr; vaginal + intrauterine balloon; each inflated up to 80 mL saline; dual mechanical pressure
Seaweed or synthetic hygroscopic osmotic rods; inserted into cervix, expand slowly; alternative mechanical method
Must be done before diagnosing 'failed induction'; mechanical stretching of lower uterine segment stimulates PG release

blocks.text: 2006 US survey: 73% of US obstetricians would routinely induce low-risk women at 41 wk
9. Management Algorithm

Management of Post-Term Pregnancies — Algorithm (Fig. 21.2, Fanaroff 2025)
40 weeks: เริ่ม daily fetal kick counts + biweekly modified BPP; Consider induction if cervix favorable. 41 weeks: Continue kick counts + biweekly modified BPP; Membrane stripping; Recommend induction especially toward end of week. 42 weeks: Induction/Delivery. Induction/delivery indicated any time: reduced fetal movements OR abnormal fetal testing. Dilemma: expectant management → lower cesarean risk; BUT ↑ maternal/perinatal complications from advanced GA → ต้อง individualize.
9.1 Key Trials Comparing Induction vs Expectant Management
Retrospective study (41+ wk): induction vs expectant management → no difference in cesarean or operative vaginal delivery rate

RCT (n=1801; induction vs fetal surveillance from 41 wk): 1 fetal death (0.1%) induction vs 2 (0.2%) expectant; composite adverse perinatal outcome 1.7% induction vs 3.1% expectant (marginally significant); cesarean rate similar (10.7% both groups)
No consensus on optimal test type, frequency, or combination — evidence base limited
10. NICU Practical — รับ Post-Term Neonate
ก่อนคลอด — communicate กับ OB: GA (ยืนยัน EDD); LMP vs US dating; meconium ใน fluid?; fetal surveillance result; reason for delivery/induction

Delivery room — meconium protocol: ถ้า meconium-stained AF: ไม่ suction routine ทันที หลัง NRP 2015 — assess tone/breathing/HR แรก; ถ้า vigorous (good tone + cry + HR >100) → routine care; ถ้า depressed → intubate + suction via ETT ก่อน PPV (NRP 2015 update remains controversial — check current local protocol)
Clifford syndrome — skin care: dry cracked skin → moisturizer; protect skin integrity; watch for infection through cracked skin
Glucose: serial monitoring; post-term macrosomic = ↑ insulin → hypoglycemia risk; target >2.6 mmol/L (46 mg/dL); IV dextrose ถ้าไม่ตอบสนองต่อ enteral feeding
Polycythemia: venous Hct Day 1–2; ≥65% + symptomatic (plethora, tachycardia, lethargy, ↓ glucose) → partial exchange transfusion with NS
Birth injuries — comprehensive exam: shoulder dystocia หลังคลอด → ตรวจ Moro reflex symmetry; arm position; clavicle tenderness (crepitus); facial nerve palsy; CT/XR ถ้าสงสัย
HIE — cooling criteria: post-term neonate ≥36 wk + perinatal asphyxia → assess TOBY/NICHD criteria; start within 6 hr ถ้าเข้าเกณฑ์
PPHN + MAS: ถ้า MAS → surfactant (↓ surface tension + surfactant inactivation); iNO สำหรับ PPHN; HFOV ถ้า conventional ventilation ไม่พอ; ECMO criteria ถ้า refractory
Sepsis workup: prolonged pregnancy + chorioamnionitis → ↑ early-onset sepsis; low threshold; GBS prophylaxis status จาก OB
Family counseling: แจ้ง long-term risks: behavioral/emotional problems 2x; CP risk ↑; obesity risk; developmental follow-up → refer developmental pediatrics
11. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Meconium ≠ Automatic Intubation: NRP 2015 Nuance
Post-term + meconium-stained AF: ถ้า newborn vigorous (good tone + cry + HR >100) → ไม่ต้อง intubate routine; observe + suction if needed. ถ้า depressed (↓ tone, ↓ cry, HR <100) → intubate + suction below cords ก่อน PPV. Thick meconium + birth asphyxia = high MAS risk → senior physician at delivery. อย่า assume 'post-term = always intubate' — assess clinical status ก่อน.

PEARL 2 — Clifford Syndrome: Recognize and Anticipate Complications
Dry cracked parchment skin + long meconium-stained fingernails + absent vernix + 'old worried face' = Clifford postmaturity syndrome. ชัดเจนตั้งแต่ 1954. Anticipate: (1) ↑ hypothermia — ↓ subcut fat; (2) ↑ hypoglycemia — depleted glycogen; (3) ↑ infection — skin barrier disrupted; (4) ↑ MAS risk. Document stigmata ตั้งแต่แรกรับเพื่อ counseling family.

PEARL 3 — Polycythemia ใน Post-Term: Same Mechanism as FGR
Post-term hypoxia (↓ placental O₂) → ↑ EPO (cord blood EPO ↑ 67%) → ↑ RBC production → Hct ≥65% = polycythemia. Features: plethora, tachycardia, ↓ glucose, ↑ viscosity → ↓ cerebral/renal/gut perfusion. Check venous Hct (not capillary) Days 1–2. Symptomatic + Hct ≥65%: partial exchange with 0.9% NS (N saline — not albumin).

PEARL 4 — Perinatal Mortality Inflects at ≥42 wk: 4–5x Jump
US data 12 million births 2014–2016: stillbirths/1000 = 0.61 (39 wk), 0.56 (40 wk), 0.55 (41 wk) → 2.664 (≥42 wk). Post-term mortality ≠ linear — it's nonlinear with a sharp inflection. นี่คือ rationale สำหรับ induction ที่ 41–42 wk แม้จะดูเหมือน 'elective'. เมื่อ OB ถาม neo: 'ทารก post-term ควร deliver ไหม?' — ตอบ: กลไก placental dysfunction + mortality data support induction no later than 42 wk.

PEARL 5 — Long-Term Follow-Up: Post-Term ≠ Normal After Discharge
Parents มักคิดว่า 'ทารกครบกำหนด = ปกติ'. แต่ post-term = ↑ behavioral problems 2x ที่อายุ 1.5–3 ปี, ↑ anxiety/substance use ใน adult life, ↑ obesity (especially boys), CP RR 1.44. แจ้ง family ตั้งแต่ NICU: developmental follow-up plan สำคัญ. เขียนใน discharge summary: 'post-term gestation ≥42 wk — high-risk neurodevelopmental follow-up warranted'.
12. Evidence Update — Key Studies
ACOG Committee Opinion No. 579 Obstet Gynecol 2013;122:1139: definition of term pregnancy — classification early/full/late/post-term from 37 wk
Reddy UM et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2011;117:1279: term pregnancy heterogeneous risk for infant mortality; lowest risk 39 0/7–40 6/7 wk — foundational for current classification
Bennett KA et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2004;190:1077 (RCT): first-trimester US screening → significant reduction in post-term labor induction rates
ACOG Committee Opinion No. 700 Obstet Gynecol 2017;129:e150: methods estimating due date; adjust LMP if first-trimester US discrepancy ≥6 days
Oberg AS et al Am J Epidemiol 2013;177:531: Swedish n=475,429 — familial clustering post-term; ~50% variation attributable to genetic factors; sisters of post-term women at ↑ risk
Schierding W et al J Med Genet 2018;55:55 (GWAS): Northern Finland cohorts n=9,141; 1,167 post-term births → ADAMTS13 intronic gene variant associated with post-term
Margerison-Zilko CE et al Evol Med Public Health 2015: September 11 stress study → ↑ post-term delivery odds in gestations 33–36 wk exposed to stress
Slack E et al BMC Pregnancy Childbirth 2019;19:434: maternal obesity classes → direct correlation with absolute post-term birth risk; n=479,864 English births
Alexander JM et al Obstet Gynecol 2000;96:291: n>50,000 births; macrosomia ↑ 8→12→15% at 40→41→42 wk; cesarean ↑ 7→14%
Po G et al Am J Obstet Gynecol 2020;222:88: perinatal mortality 12M US births 2014–2016; stillbirths/1000: 0.613 (39 wk), 0.561 (40 wk), 0.550 (41 wk), 2.664 (≥42 wk) — sharp inflection

Maoz O et al J Matern Fetal Neonatal Med 2019;32:1847: immediate perinatal outcomes post-term: ↑ NICU, ↑ respiratory morbidity, ↑ infections, ↑ hypoglycemia; no ↑ birth trauma/neurologic morbidity in well-monitored group
Gottlieb AG & Galan HL Obstet Gynecol Clin North Am 2007;34:501: shoulder dystocia review: post-term = known risk factor; management algorithm; nerve injury classification
Zeitlin J et al BJOG 2007;114:1097: European post-term rates: Denmark/Sweden 7–8%; Austria 0.4% — variation reflects different management approaches and dating
ACOG Practice Bulletin No. 146, 2014: management of late-term and postterm pregnancies — surveillance, induction timing, methods
13. Self-Check Q&A
Q: Post-term pregnancy หมายถึงอะไร? ต่างจาก late term อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Post-term: ≥42 0/7 wk (≥294 days from LMP). Late term: 41 0/7–41 6/7 wk. Full term: 39 0/7–40 6/7 wk (lowest adverse outcomes). ACOG Committee Opinion 579 (2013) กำหนด classification ชัดเจน — ห้ามใช้ 'postdates', 'prolonged', 'postmature'.
Q: ทำไม LMP ไม่น่าเชื่อถือสำหรับ GA dating? และ first-trimester US ช่วยได้อย่างไร?

ตอบ: LMP ขึ้นกับ (1) maternal recall; (2) cycle regularity; (3) ovulation timing — ไม่แน่นอนทั้ง 3 อย่าง. 6–40% ของ women มีการปรับ EDD หลัง first-trimester US (Bennett 2004). First-trimester US (crown-rump length): ปรับ EDD ถ้า discrepancy ≥6 วัน (ACOG COO 700, 2017); ลด over-diagnosis post-term; ลด unnecessary induction.
Q: Clifford postmaturity syndrome มีลักษณะอะไรบ้าง? Neonatologist ต้องเตรียมรับอะไร?

ตอบ: Clifford 1954: absent vernix; dry/cracked/parchment skin; long meconium-stained nails; 'old and worried' appearance; alert open eyes; scaphoid abdomen (↓ fat). Prepare: (1) ↑ MAS risk — meconium protocol; (2) ↑ hypoglycemia — serial glucose; (3) ↑ hypothermia — warm delivery room; (4) ↑ polycythemia — Hct Day 1–2; (5) skin breakdown → infection risk.
Q: Perinatal mortality ใน post-term ≥42 wk เป็นเท่าไร เทียบกับ 39–41 wk?

ตอบ: จากข้อมูล 12 million US births 2014–2016 (Po 2020): 39 wk = 0.613/1000; 40 wk = 0.561; 41 wk = 0.550 — ค่อนข้างคงที่. ≥42 wk = 2.664/1000 — jump ~4–5x อย่างชัดเจน. นี่คือ rationale สำหรับ mandatory delivery ที่ 42 wk.
Q: X-linked placental sulfatase deficiency เกี่ยวข้องกับ post-term อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Placental sulfatase → converts sulfated estrogen precursors → active estrogens → cervical ripening + labor initiation. Deficiency (X-linked recessive) → ↓ estrogen synthesis → cervix ไม่ ripen → ↓ uterine contractility → prolonged gestation. Also associated with adrenal hypoplasia (↓ fetal DHEAS). Risk factor ใน Chapter 21.
Q: Modified BPP คืออะไร? ต่างจาก BPP เต็มอย่างไร และทำไม preferred สำหรับ post-term surveillance?

ตอบ: Modified BPP: NST + AFV assessment เท่านั้น. BPP เต็ม: NST + 4 US components (tone, gross movement, breathing, AFV) = 10 pts. Modified BPP ง่ายกว่า เร็วกว่า — RCT after 42 wk: BPP มี more abnormal results เพราะใช้ AFI (ไม่แม่น) แทน DVP; modified BPP preferred. Normal = reactive NST + AFV normal (DVP ≥2 cm หรือ AFI ≥5 cm).
Q: Long-term behavioral/psychiatric outcomes ของ post-term neonate มีอะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: Behavioral problems 2x more common ที่ 1.5 + 3 yr (attention/hyperactivity + emotional); ↑ risk mental disorders (substance use, anxiety disorders) — especially men; ↑ obesity/higher BMI ใน boys (continues to worsen with age); ↓ IQ ~2 pts (small effect); CP RR 1.44 after ≥42 wk; intellectual disability ↑.
Q: Foley bulb catheter ใช้อย่างไรใน cervical ripening? ต่างจาก cervical ripening balloon อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Foley bulb: 24–26 Fr; สอดเข้า uterus; inflate extra-amniotic balloon ด้วย 30–80 mL saline → mechanical pressure → ripen cervix. Cervical ripening balloon: 18 Fr, double lumen (vaginal + intrauterine); แต่ละ balloon inflate ≤80 mL saline → dual mechanical pressure. ทั้งคู่เป็น mechanical ripening — ไม่ใช่ pharmacologic; ใช้ได้ใน prior uterine scar.
Q: Management algorithm ของ post-term: ควรทำอะไรที่ 40, 41, 42 สัปดาห์?

ตอบ: 40 wk: daily fetal kick counts + biweekly modified BPP; consider induction if cervix favorable. 41 wk: continue kick counts + modified BPP; membrane stripping; strongly recommend induction toward end of week. 42 wk: induction/delivery without delay. Induction anytime: ↓ fetal movement หรือ abnormal fetal testing.
Q: Post-term neonate มี polycythemia เพราะอะไร? วิธีจัดการ?

ตอบ: Chronic placental dysfunction → ↓ fetal oxygenation → ↑ EPO → ↑ RBC production → Hct ≥65%. Cord blood EPO ↑ 67% ใน post-term pregnancies. Symptomatic (plethora, tachycardia, ↓ glucose, irritability) + venous Hct ≥65% → partial exchange transfusion with 0.9% NS — ไม่ใช่ albumin (no evidence benefit + cost/risk).

Day 119 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.22
บทต่อไป: Chapter 22 — Immune and Nonimmune Hydrops Fetalis. เตรียมตัว: definition hydrops (fluid ≥2 serous cavities); immune hydrops (Rh alloimmunization — anti-D mechanism, Coombs test) vs nonimmune (cardiovascular/lymphatic/anemia/infection/metabolic causes); prenatal diagnosis (fetal US, MCA-PSV for fetal anemia); management (intrauterine transfusion); delivery room stabilization; postnatal management ของ severe hydrops (ascites, pleural effusion, skin edema); prognosis ตาม etiology.
